CheckTitle: Okta uses active Network Zone blocklists for anonymized proxy sources
Description: Okta should use an active Network Zone blocklist before authentication. Enhanced Dynamic Zone anonymizer categories provide direct anonymizer coverage; static IP blocklists provide evidence that gateway/proxy IP entries are blocked, but Prowler cannot verify full anonymizer-provider coverage from static entries alone.
Risk: If anonymized proxy sources are not blocked before authentication, attackers can hide source networks and attempt credential attacks or session establishment from untrusted infrastructure.

Code.Other: Security > Networks: configure BlockedIpZone gateway/proxy IP entries or activate DefaultEnhancedDynamicZone / Enhanced Dynamic Zone blocklisting for anonymizers.

Recommendation.Text: Prefer an active Enhanced Dynamic Zone blocklist that covers anonymizers. If using a static IP Network Zone blocklist, keep gateway/proxy entries actively maintained because Prowler cannot verify full anonymizer-provider coverage from static entries alone.
